playbooks.title: High Risk Customer Retention
playbooks.content: For customers with risk scores above 70, prioritize immediate outreach. Offer a loyalty discount of up to 20% or a dedicated success manager. Schedule a quarterly business review within 2 weeks.

playbooks.title: Enterprise Cross-Sell Strategy
playbooks.content: For enterprise customers (Revenue > $7M) using only 1-2 products, recommend 'Corporate Credit Line' or 'Trade Finance Solution' as the next best product. Highlight volume-based pricing advantages.

playbooks.title: Growth Segment Upsell
playbooks.content: For growth segment customers ($3M-$7M revenue), suggest upgrading to the 'Premium Business Package' tier to ensure scalability. Focus on value realization and ROI stories.

playbooks.title: Onboarding Acceleration
playbooks.content: New customers in their first 90 days should complete product activation, attend a kickoff call, and set success milestones. Assign a dedicated onboarding specialist for accounts over $2M.

playbooks.title: Renewal Risk Mitigation
playbooks.content: For accounts renewing within 90 days with health scores below 60, initiate a 'Save Play': executive sponsor call, custom success plan, and potential contract incentive. Escalate to VP if NPS < 5.

playbooks.title: Support Escalation Protocol
playbooks.content: Accounts with 3+ open support tickets require immediate triage. CSM should join the next support call, create a resolution timeline, and provide daily status updates until tickets are below 2.

playbooks.title: Champion Expansion Playbook
playbooks.content: For Champion-stage customers (health > 85, NPS > 8), pursue case study opportunities, referral programs, and multi-year contract discussions. These accounts are ideal for beta feature access.

playbooks.title: Declining Usage Intervention
playbooks.content: When usage trend shows 'declining' for 2+ months, schedule a usage review meeting. Identify adoption blockers, offer training sessions, and consider assigning a product specialist.

faq.question: What are the eligibility rules for the loyalty discount?
faq.answer: Customers must have been with the platform for over 2 years and have a risk level of High or a health score below 40.

faq.question: When should a customer be escalated to VP-level attention?
faq.answer: Escalate when: NPS score is below 4, renewal is within 60 days with declining health, or there are 5+ open support tickets.

faq.question: What defines a Champion customer?
faq.answer: A Champion has a health score above 85, NPS of 8+, growing usage trend, and has been on the platform for at least 12 months.

faq.question: How is customer health score calculated?
faq.answer: Health score combines: product adoption (30%), support satisfaction (20%), usage frequency (25%), NPS (15%), and revenue trend (10%).

product_documentation.summary: Flexible revolving credit facility for enterprise operations. Ideal for companies with revenue > $7M needing working capital flexibility.
Proactive risk management with real-time early-warning alerts, quarterly health assessments, and dedicated risk advisory.
Support for international trade and supply-chain payments. Best for manufacturing and technology companies with cross-border operations.
Short-term financing for day-to-day operations. Designed for emerging businesses needing cash flow support.
Everyday business spending with cashback rewards and expense tracking. Entry-level product for all segments.

CUST001.note: Customer expressed concern about recent downtime. Moderate churn risk.
Renewed primary contract, but opted out of expansion.
Quarterly review completed. Satisfied with core services.

CUST002.note: Manufacturing output declining. Customer exploring competitor options.
VP-level escalation: customer threatened to leave after billing dispute.
Resolution in progress. Offered 15% loyalty discount.
Emergency review call. Customer remains dissatisfied with response time.

CUST003.note: Excellent product adoption. Expressed interest in expanding to corporate credit line.
Strategic planning session. Agreed to explore multi-year contract.
Positive quarterly check-in. Requested beta access to new analytics features.

CUST004.note: Onboarding delayed due to internal restructuring at MediCare.
3 support tickets opened regarding integration issues.
CSM intervention call. Assigned dedicated onboarding specialist.

CUST005.note: Top-tier customer. Using all major products effectively.
Executive review: discussing case study and referral program.
Confirmed interest in becoming a platform reference customer.

CUST006.note: Revenue declining sharply. Multiple missed payments.
Risk team alerted. Customer facing cash flow crisis.
Last contact attempt. Customer unresponsive to outreach.

CUST007.note: Won major government construction contract. Revenue expected to grow 40%.
Discussed equipment finance expansion for new project fleet.
Follow-up on trade finance proposal. Decision expected by July.

CUST008.note: SaaS platform growing but only using basic savings account.
Competitor outreach detected. Need to accelerate cross-sell.
Discussed trade finance and corporate credit options.

CUST009.note: Education sector facing budget cuts. Customer may downgrade services.
5 tickets opened. Primary complaint: slow response times.

CUST010.note: New customer onboarded. Agriculture sector with seasonal revenue patterns.
Kickoff meeting completed. Set 90-day success milestones.
30-day check-in. Progressing well on adoption milestones.
